title: Breaking Down ‘I’m Thinking of Ending Things,’ With Charlie Kaufman
format: Sean and Amanda are joined by critic Amy Nicholson to argue about Charlie Kaufman's Netflix film 'I'm Thinking of Ending Things,' then Sean sits down with Kaufman and novelist Ian Reid.

segments.heading: Charlie Kaufman and ‘I’m Thinking of Ending Things’

films.title: I'm Thinking of Ending Things
films.note: The main event: Kaufman's adaptation of Ian Reid's novel, dropped on Netflix. The table splits over whether to solve it as a puzzle or, as Amy argues, just let it wash over you like a moving painting, and over whether Jessie Buckley's character is a real woman or a projection of the old man's regrets.

films.title: Being John Malkovich
films.note: The Spike Jonze breakout Sean calls one of his favorite movies of the last 50 years, and his own gateway into deconstructing a text.

films.title: Adaptation
films.note: Amanda's top-five movie about making movies; she saw it in college and it taught her how far you can push a piece of writing.

films.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
films.note: The Michel Gondry collaboration where, as Amy puts it, Kaufman's cynicism and Gondry's romanticism fight in the soul of the film. Also cited as the source of Kaufman's manic-pixie problem.

films.title: Synecdoche, New York
films.note: His directorial debut and Amy's top-two film of all time. She compares it to Pennies from Heaven and saw it on Thanksgiving with her family.

films.title: Anomalisa
films.note: The stop-motion film he co-directed from his own radio play; Kaufman doesn't fully count it as one of his movies. Every character but two shares David Thewlis's voice.

films.title: Human Nature
films.note: Brought up for Patricia Arquette's character as another Kaufman lead built around public performance.

films.title: I'm Thinking of Ending Things
films.note: Kaufman says he went looking for something to adapt to improve his odds of getting a film financed, and Amazon recommended Reid's book. He explains changing the ending to give the woman real agency and resistance, and calls the film his likely swan song, his first movie made since 2008.

films.title: Under the Skin
films.note: Reid's last-great-thing pick: he loves both Michel Faber's novel and Jonathan Glazer's film precisely because they're so different. Sean notes he profiled Glazer and never got a satisfying answer about the gap between them.

Books: I'm Thinking of Ending Things (Ian Reid novel)
A Supposedly Fun Thing I'll Never Do Again (David Foster Wallace)

Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
